title: Fix Start Game flow and ESC pause menu behavior

requirements.summary: Ensure clicking 'Start Game' from the main menu launches directly into active gameplay with no pause overlay or ESC menu visible
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Start Game' from MainMenu transitions immediately to the game screen with PauseOverlay hidden.
No pause state is active when the game first starts.
The player can move and interact immediately upon entering the game.

file_operations.description: Ensure that when transitioning to the 'game' screen from the main menu, any existing pause state is cleared and the game starts in an active (unpaused) state. Verify that the screen transition does not inadvertently trigger pause logic.

Modify the GameScene component to initialize with isPaused set to false when the game first loads. Ensure the PauseOverlay is only rendered when isPaused is true. Add logic to prevent the ESC key from toggling pause before the game has fully started (e.g., during initial countdown or menu phase).
Ensure the PauseOverlay component is conditionally rendered only when the pause state is active. Verify that the overlay does not render on initial game load.

requirements.summary: Implement ESC key pause/resume toggle that freezes gameplay (enemies, timers) until the player clicks resume or presses ESC again
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing ESC during active gameplay sets a paused state to true and renders the PauseOverlay.
While paused, enemy movement, wave timers, and player actions are frozen.
Clicking the resume button in PauseOverlay sets paused state to false and resumes all game systems.
Pressing ESC again while paused resumes the game (toggle behavior).
ESC key has no effect on the pause menu when the player is in the main menu or game-over screen.

file_operations.description: Add an ESC key event listener that toggles the isPaused state during active gameplay. Ensure the listener is only active when the current screen is 'game' and the wave phase is 'active'. Pass the isPaused state to all child systems (enemy system, wave system, weapon system) to freeze their logic. Wire the PauseOverlay's onResume callback to set isPaused to false.

Accept an isPaused boolean parameter. When isPaused is true, skip all enemy movement, attack, and spawn logic in the frame loop. Ensure enemy state is preserved while paused and resumes correctly when unpaused.

Accept an isPaused boolean parameter. When isPaused is true, pause all countdown timers (wave start, wave cleared) and freeze the active wave phase. Ensure timers resume from their paused position when isPaused becomes false.
Accept an isPaused boolean parameter. When isPaused is true, disable weapon firing, reloading, and switching. Ignore all input events (mouse clicks, keyboard shortcuts) while paused.
Accept an isPaused boolean parameter. When isPaused is true, disable player movement (WASD, jump, sprint) and mouse look. Lock the camera and player position while paused.
